{"id":"smoke-gp1","title":"Project Scaffolding: Electron + Vite + React + TypeScript","description":"Set up the complete project skeleton using electron-vite as the build tool with React + TypeScript.

## What to Build

Initialize the Electron app with three-process architecture (main, preload, renderer) using electron-vite for fast HMR.

## Dependencies to Install

**dependencies:** react, react-dom, @xterm/xterm, @xterm/addon-webgl, @xterm/addon-fit, node-pty, electron-store, uuid, zustand
**devDependencies:** electron, electron-vite, @electron/rebuild, electron-builder, typescript, @types/react, @types/react-dom, @types/uuid, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react

## Key Files to Create

- package.json — with all deps, scripts (dev, build, start, rebuild), and postinstall for electron-rebuild
- electron.vite.config.ts — externalize node-pty (native C++ addon can't be bundled by Vite), configure react plugin for renderer

- src/renderer/index.html, main.tsx, App.tsx — React 18 createRoot, render Hello Smoke

- node-pty MUST be externalized from Vite bundle (it's a native .node addon)

- BrowserWindow sandbox must be false so preload can use Node APIs for the IPC bridge

- Main process hot-reloads on change
- node-pty can be imported in main process without error
- TypeScript compilation succeeds with strict mode
- npm run build produces a working production build","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T13:15:59Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-14T13:15:59Z"}

{"id":"smoke-n5y","title":"PTY Backend: Main Process PTY Management + IPC","description":"Implement the PTY management layer in the Electron main process. This is the core backend that spawns shell processes, pipes data bidirectionally over IPC, handles resizing, and manages process lifecycle.

## What to Build

### src/main/pty/PtyProcess.ts
Wraps a single node-pty instance:
- Constructor takes: id (string), cwd (string), shell (optional, defaults to platform default), args (optional), env (optional), cols (default 80), rows (default 24)
- Determine shell: options.shell || process.env.SHELL (mac) || 'powershell.exe' (win)
- Spawn via ptySpawn(shell, args, { name: 'xterm-256color', cols, rows, cwd, env: { ...process.env, ...env } })
- Emit 'data' events on pty output, 'exit' events on process exit
- Methods: write(data), resize(cols, rows), kill()
- Track exited state to prevent writes to dead processes

### src/main/pty/PtyManager.ts
Manages all PTY instances:
- Map<string, PtyProcess> for storage
- Methods: spawn(options) -> PtyProcess, get(id), write(id, data), resize(id, cols, rows), kill(id), killAll()
- killAll() called on app quit to prevent zombie processes

### src/main/ipc/channels.ts
Channel constants and TypeScript message type definitions:
- PTY_SPAWN: 'pty:spawn' (invoke/handle - request/response)
- PTY_DATA_TO_PTY: 'pty:data:to-pty' (one-way, renderer -> main)
- PTY_DATA_FROM_PTY: 'pty:data:from-pty' (one-way, main -> renderer)
- PTY_RESIZE: 'pty:resize' (one-way)
- PTY_KILL: 'pty:kill' (one-way)
- PTY_EXIT: 'pty:exit' (one-way, main -> renderer)

### src/main/ipc/ipcHandlers.ts
- pty:spawn — use ipcMain.handle (request/response, renderer needs the result)
- pty:data:to-pty — use ipcMain.on (fire-and-forget for keystroke performance)
- pty:resize — use ipcMain.on
- pty:kill — use ipcMain.on
- PTY output and exit events sent via mainWindow.webContents.send

### src/preload/index.ts
Expose typed smokeAPI.pty object via contextBridge:
- spawn(options) -> ipcRenderer.invoke('pty:spawn', options)
- write(id, data) -> ipcRenderer.send('pty:data:to-pty', { id, data })
- resize(id, cols, rows) -> ipcRenderer.send('pty:resize', { id, cols, rows })
- kill(id) -> ipcRenderer.send('pty:kill', { id })
- onData(callback) -> subscribe to 'pty:data:from-pty', return unsubscribe function
- onExit(callback) -> subscribe to 'pty:exit', return unsubscribe function

### src/preload/types.ts
TypeScript type definitions for the smokeAPI that both preload and renderer can reference.

## Critical Performance Decision
Use ipcMain.on (NOT handle) for pty:data:to-pty because keystrokes must be fire-and-forget with zero await overhead. Use webContents.send for PTY output (not invoke response). Only pty:spawn uses handle/invoke.

## Cleanup
Register app.on('before-quit', () => ptyManager.killAll()) in main/index.ts.

## Platform Detection
getDefaultShell(): process.platform === 'win32' ? (process.env.COMSPEC || 'powershell.exe') : (process.env.SHELL || '/bin/bash')

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can spawn a PTY from renderer via window.smokeAPI.pty.spawn({ id, cwd })
- Keystrokes sent via write() appear in the PTY
- PTY output streams back to renderer via onData callback
- resize() changes terminal dimensions (verified with stty size)
- kill() terminates the process and triggers onExit
- All PTYs killed on app quit (no zombie processes)
- Works on macOS (zsh/bash) and Windows (powershell/cmd)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T13:16:24Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-14T13:16:24Z"}

This is the spatial container for all terminal windows. ``` ## What to Build ### src/renderer/canvas/useCanvasControls.ts (MOST PERFORMANCE-CRITICAL) Manages pan/zoom entirely through refs and direct DOM manipulation, bypassing React reconciliation: - panRef, zoomRef, isPanningRef, lastPointerRef — all useRef, NO useState - applyTransform() — directly sets viewportRef.current.style.transform = translate3d(x,y,0) scale(z) - WHEEL handler: scroll to pan (deltaX/deltaY), ctrl/meta+scroll to zoom toward cursor - MIDDLE MOUSE / SPACE+DRAG: pan with pointer capture - Zoom-to-cursor math: newPanX = cursorX - (cursorX - oldPanX) * (newZoom / oldZoom) - Zoom clamp: 0.1 to 3.0 - On pan-end/zoom-end: sync values to Zustand canvasStore (debounced) for persistence - All event listeners added with { passive: false } for wheel to allow preventDefault ### src/renderer/canvas/Grid.tsx SVG repeating pattern for grid lines: - Uses element for efficiency (not individual lines) - Grid scales naturally with viewport transform (it's a child of viewport div) - Fade out when zoom < 0.3: opacity = Math.min(1, (zoom - 0.3) * 2) - Return null when zoom < 0.3 (not just invisible — remove from DOM) - Pattern: M gridSize 0 L 0 0 0 gridSize with rgba(255,255,255,0.06) stroke ### src/renderer/canvas/Canvas.tsx Outer component: - Contains canvas-root div and viewport ref - Calls useCanvasControls(viewportRef) - Maps sessions to TerminalWindow components (placeholder divs for now) - Gets sessions from sessionStore ### src/renderer/styles/canvas.css ```css .canvas-root { position: relative; {"id":"smoke-3wi","title":"Terminal Rendering: xterm.js + WebGL on Canvas","description":"Mount xterm.js terminals inside canvas-positioned divs and connect them to the PTY backend via IPC. Handles xterm.js lifecycle, WebGL renderer setup, and the critical CSS-transform compatibility workaround.

## Critical Known Issue

xterm.js CharSizeService uses getBoundingClientRect() which returns dimensions AFTER CSS transforms. When the canvas is scaled (zoomed), xterm.js computes wrong character sizes, causing layout bugs.

WORKAROUND: Initialize xterm.js terminals at scale=1. When canvas zoom changes, do NOT rely on FitAddon auto-sizing. Set terminal dimensions (cols/rows) explicitly based on the LOGICAL (pre-transform) size of the container. Measure character dimensions once at creation, cache them.

```typescript
function calculateTerminalSize(widthPx: number, heightPx: number, charWidth: number, charHeight: number) {
  return {
    cols: Math.max(2, Math.floor(widthPx / charWidth)),
    rows: Math.max(1, Math.floor(heightPx / charHeight))
  };
}
```

## What to Build

### src/renderer/terminal/useTerminal.ts

xterm.js lifecycle hook:
- Creates Terminal instance with: cursorBlink: true, fontFamily: 'Menlo, Monaco, "Courier New", monospace', fontSize: 13, lineHeight: 1.2, scrollback: 10000, allowTransparency: false (faster), theme: { background: '#1a1a2e', foreground: '#e0e0e0' }
- Loads FitAddon (for initial sizing only)
- Opens terminal into container ref
- Loads WebglAddon AFTER open (requires canvas context). Handle onContextLoss by disposing WebGL addon (xterm falls back to canvas renderer automatically).
- Stores terminalRef, fitAddonRef, webglAddonRef as useRef
- Cleanup: terminal.dispose() on unmount
- Expose getSnapshot() method: captures last N lines from terminal buffer for thumbnail mode

### src/renderer/terminal/usePty.ts

IPC bridge hook connecting xterm.js to PTY backend:
- Subscribe to window.smokeAPI.pty.onData — filter by sessionId, call terminal.write(data)
- Subscribe to terminal.onData — call window.smokeAPI.pty.write(sessionId, data)
- Subscribe to window.smokeAPI.pty.onExit — display exit message in terminal, update session status
- Return cleanup functions for all subscriptions

Performance note: onData callback receives events for ALL sessions, each terminal filters by event.id === sessionId. This is O(N) per event. If N>20 terminals becomes a bottleneck, refactor to centralized dispatcher with Map<id, callback> for O(1) lookup. Start simple.

### src/renderer/terminal/TerminalWidget.tsx

Mount point component:
- Renders a div ref as container
- Calls useTerminal(containerRef, sessionId, cols, rows)
- Calls usePty(sessionId, terminalRef)
- CSS class "terminal-container" with width/height 100%

### src/renderer/styles/terminal.css

```css
.terminal-container {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.terminal-container .xterm {
  height: 100%;
  padding: 4px;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- xterm.js terminal renders inside a canvas-positioned div
- Typing sends keystrokes to PTY and output appears
- WebGL renderer active (verify: single canvas element, no xterm-text-layer)
- Full scrollback works (scroll up to see history)
- Terminal renders correctly at different canvas zoom levels (no character size distortion)
- onContextLoss gracefully falls back to canvas renderer
- Process exit displayed in terminal with exit code
- 10000 lines of scrollback preserved","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T13:17:41Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-14T13:17:41Z"}

Tests: 8 unit tests for calculateTerminalSize. DECISIONS: Inlined calculateTerminalSize test logic to avoid importing browser-dependent xterm modules in vitest."}

{"id":"smoke-pk8","title":"Window Management: Drag, Resize, Snap, Chrome","description":"Implement window chrome (title bar, close button) around each terminal, along with drag-to-move and drag-to-resize with grid snapping.

## What to Build

### src/renderer/terminal/TerminalWindow.tsx

Wraps TerminalWidget with window chrome and interaction handlers:
- Positioned absolutely on canvas: left/top from session.position, width/height from session.size
- z-index from session.zIndex
- CSS classes for focused, highlighted (sidebar hover), dragging states
- onPointerDown calls bringToFront(session.id)
- Contains: WindowChrome (top), terminal-body (middle, contains TerminalWidget), ResizeHandles (edges)

### src/renderer/window/useWindowDrag.ts

Drag state machine:
- onPointerDown on title bar: stopPropagation (prevent canvas pan), capture pointer, record start position + mouse position
- onPointerMove: calculate delta, DIVIDE BY ZOOM (critical — 100px mouse move at 0.5x zoom = 200px canvas move). Update session position in real-time via sessionStore.updateSession.
- onPointerUp: snap to grid — Math.round(position / gridSize) * gridSize. Animate snap with CSS transition (150ms ease-out).
- The zoom value must be passed in as a parameter. Getting this wrong causes drift/acceleration bugs.

### src/renderer/window/useWindowResize.ts

Resize state machine:
- Similar to drag but modifies width/height
- Resize handles on: right (e), bottom (s), bottom-right (se)
- Mouse deltas divided by zoom (same as drag)
- On resize-end: snap dimensions to grid, recalculate terminal cols/rows using calculateTerminalSize(snappedWidth, snappedHeight - CHROME_HEIGHT, charWidth, charHeight), call window.smokeAPI.pty.resize(sessionId, cols, rows)
- Minimum size: 10 grid cells wide, 8 grid cells tall (with default 20px grid = 200x160px minimum)

### src/renderer/window/useSnapping.ts

Grid snap utilities:
- snap(value) -> Math.round(value / gridSize) * gridSize
- snapPosition(pos) -> { x: snap(pos.x), y: snap(pos.y) }
- snapSize(size) -> { width: max(minWidth, snap(width)), height: max(minHeight, snap(height)) }
- During drag: calculate nearest grid lines, render thin guide lines at snap positions (visual feedback)

### src/renderer/window/WindowChrome.tsx

Title bar component:
- Status dot (green=running, gray=exited) 
- Session title (editable on double-click)
- Close button (x)
- Drag handlers attached to the chrome div (title bar is the drag handle)
- Height: 32px (CHROME_HEIGHT constant)

### src/renderer/window/ResizeHandle.tsx

Edge/corner resize handle:
- Positions: 'e' (right edge), 's' (bottom edge), 'se' (bottom-right corner)
- Renders as invisible hit areas (8px wide) with appropriate cursor styles
- On hover: show subtle visual indicator

## Critical: Zoom Compensation

ALL mouse deltas during drag and resize MUST be divided by the current canvas zoom factor:
```
canvasDeltaX = mouseDeltaX / zoom
canvasDeltaY = mouseDeltaY / zoom
```
Without this, windows drift faster at high zoom and slower at low zoom.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Terminal windows have title bar with title, status dot, and close button
- Dragging title bar moves window on canvas
- Drag works correctly at ALL zoom levels (no drift or acceleration)
- Releasing drag snaps window to nearest grid position (with smooth 150ms animation)
- Resize handles on right, bottom, and bottom-right edges
- Resizing snaps to grid on release
- Resizing triggers PTY resize (verify with stty size inside terminal)
- Minimum window size enforced (can't resize smaller than 200x160 with default grid)
- Clicking a window brings it to front (z-index updates)
- Dragging a window does NOT pan the canvas (event propagation stopped)
- Double-clicking title edits session title","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T13:18:08Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-14T13:18:08Z"}

## What to Build

### Session Creation (3 entry points)

1. Sidebar "+ New" button — creates terminal at center of current viewport
2. Double-click empty canvas — creates terminal at click position (grid-snapped)
3. Cmd/Ctrl+N keyboard shortcut — creates terminal at center of current viewport

Creation flow:
1. Determine CWD: preferences.defaultCwd if set, else the directory Smoke was launched from (process.cwd() / app.getPath())
2. Generate UUID for session ID
3. Determine position: center of viewport OR click position, snap to grid
4. Spawn PTY via smokeAPI.pty.spawn({ id, cwd, shell, args })
5. Add session to sessionStore with default size (640x480, 80x24)
6. If autoLaunchClaude enabled, PTY manager sends claude command after shell init

### Canvas Double-Click Handler (in useCanvasControls.ts)

```typescript
const onDoubleClick = (e: MouseEvent) => {
  // Only on empty canvas, not on terminal windows
  if ((e.target as HTMLElement).closest('.terminal-window')) return;
  
  // Convert screen -> canvas coordinates
  const rect = canvasRoot.getBoundingClientRect();
  const canvasX = (e.clientX - rect.left - panRef.current.x) / zoomRef.current;
  const canvasY = (e.clientY - rect.top - panRef.current.y) / zoomRef.current;
  
  // Snap to grid
  const snappedX = Math.round(canvasX / gridSize) * gridSize;
  const snappedY = Math.round(canvasY / gridSize) * gridSize;
  
  createNewSession({ x: snappedX, y: snappedY });
};
```

### Focus Management

- Clicking anywhere on terminal window: sessionStore.focusSession(id) + bringToFront(id)
- ONLY the focused terminal's xterm.js instance calls terminal.focus(). All others call terminal.blur().
- This ensures keyboard input goes to exactly one terminal
- Clicking empty canvas unfocuses all terminals

1. User clicks close button OR Cmd/Ctrl+W
2. Kill PTY: smokeAPI.pty.kill(id)
3. Wait for onExit confirmation (timeout 5s, then force-remove)

When PTY exits on its own (user types 'exit'):
- Display exit message: [Process exited with code N]

### Focus Management Challenge

When a terminal is focused, keystrokes go to xterm.js -> PTY. But global shortcuts (Cmd+N etc) must still work. Solution:

1. Use xterm.js attachCustomKeyEventHandler to intercept keys BEFORE xterm processes them:

```typescript
terminal.attachCustomKeyEventHandler((event) => {
  if (event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey) {
    const shortcut = resolveShortcut(event);
    if (shortcut) {
      executeShortcut(shortcut);
      return false;  // prevent xterm from handling
    }
  }
  if (event.key === 'Escape' && event.type === 'keydown') {
    blurTerminal();
    return false;
  }
  return true;  // let xterm handle normally
});
```

2. Document-level keydown listener with { capture: true } fires in capture phase, before xterm's bubble-phase handler.

{"id":"smoke-170","title":"Cross-OS Packaging: electron-builder for Mac + Windows","description":"Configure electron-builder to produce distributable packages for macOS (.dmg) and Windows (NSIS installer). Includes native module bundling, code signing, and platform-specific handling.

## What to Build

### electron-builder config in package.json

```json
{
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.smoke.app",
    "productName": "Smoke",
    "directories": { "output": "dist" },
    "files": ["out/**/*", "node_modules/node-pty/**/*"],
    "asarUnpack": ["node_modules/node-pty/**/*"],
    "mac": {
      "category": "public.app-category.developer-tools",
      "target": [
        { "target": "dmg", "arch": ["x64", "arm64"] },
        { "target": "zip", "arch": ["x64", "arm64"] }
      ],
      "hardenedRuntime": true,
      "gatekeeperAssess": false,
      "entitlements": "build/entitlements.mac.plist",
      "entitlementsInherit": "build/entitlements.mac.plist"
    },
    "win": {
      "target": [{ "target": "nsis", "arch": ["x64"] }]
    },
    "nsis": {
      "oneClick": false,
      "perMachine": false,
      "allowToChangeInstallationDirectory": true
    },
    "afterSign": "scripts/notarize.js"
  }
}
```

### Critical: asarUnpack for node-pty

node-pty includes native .node binaries and a spawn-helper executable. These CANNOT be loaded from inside an ASAR archive. asarUnpack extracts them to app.asar.unpacked/ at install time.

### macOS Entitlements (build/entitlements.mac.plist)

Required because node-pty uses JIT compilation and unsigned memory:
- com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit: true
- com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory: true
- com.apple.security.cs.allow-dyld-environment-variables: true

### Notarization Script (scripts/notarize.js)

Uses @electron/notarize:
- Only runs on darwin
- Reads APPLE_ID, APPLE_ID_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID from env
- Notarizes the .app bundle after signing

### npm Scripts

- "package:mac": "npm run build && electron-builder --mac"
- "package:win": "npm run build && electron-builder --win"
- "package:all": "npm run build && electron-builder --mac --win"

### Platform-Specific Shell Detection

In PtyManager.ts:
```typescript
function getDefaultShell(): string {
  if (process.platform === 'win32') {
    return process.env.COMSPEC || 'powershell.exe';
  }
  return process.env.SHELL || '/bin/bash';
}
```

### Windows Considerations
- node-pty on Windows uses ConPTY (Windows 10+), falls back to winpty
- Use path.join everywhere, never hardcode /
- electron-store handles config directory automatically

### App Icons
- resources/icon.icns (macOS, 512x512 minimum)
- resources/icon.ico (Windows, multi-resolution)

- npm run package:mac produces working .dmg for macOS (x64 and arm64)
- npm run package:win produces working NSIS installer for Windows x64

- App launches and terminals work on clean Windows machine

new_value: Fixed: marked.parse() output was injected via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization, allowing XSS through malicious markdown. Added DOMPurify.sanitize() to the markdown rendering path in FileViewerWidget.tsx. Regression tests cover script injection, event handlers, javascript: URIs, iframes, and safe content preservation.

Fixed: createPluginSession was called with wrong parameter order (pluginType, title, pluginData, pos, size) instead of (pluginType, pluginId, pluginSource, manifest, pluginData, pos) in both restoreTabLayout and useLayoutRestore. Also fixed same bug in useSessionCreation duplicate handler. Added missing serialization of pluginId/pluginSource/pluginManifest, plus backward-compat fallbacks for old layouts.

Fixed: FS_READFILE and FS_READFILE_BASE64 IPC handlers were missing the assertWithinHome() path boundary check, allowing arbitrary file reads outside the user home directory. Added the same check already used by FS_WRITEFILE. Updated error message to 'Access denied'. Added regression tests for both handlers.

Fixed: request.filename was passed unsanitized to path.join() in RECORDING_LOAD and RECORDING_EXPORT handlers, enabling directory traversal. Fix: added path.basename() sanitization, reject filenames with directory components, and isWithinBoundary defense-in-depth check. 4 regression tests added.
